mechanism_narrative: A flat or falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers the discount rate on equities and supports multiple expansion, so S&P 500 returns should be positive over the next 20 trading days when the policy rate is not rising.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not positive in a majority of periods with non-rising FEDFUNDS, or if rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent level indicates deteriorating labor demand and weaker household income growth, which should reduce S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days after the threshold is crossed.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns more than 60% of the time, or if the average forward return is not lower than the unconditional mean by at least 1 standard deviation.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth together with rising industrial production indicates broad final demand and output momentum, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days because earnings revisions improve.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint signal has no incremental predictive power versus either series alone, or if 60-day forward returns are not positive after above-median RSAFS and INDPRO growth.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent rise in INDPRO reflects improving productive capacity and corporate operating leverage, which should translate into positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as output momentum feeds margins.
failure_condition: Reject if positive monthly INDPRO momentum is not followed by above-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the signal flips sign in repeated rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in HOUST signals weakening housing demand and a broader slowdown in cyclical spending, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next 20 trading days after housing starts break down.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 8% below its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if threshold-crossing episodes do not show negative average 20-day returns, or if more than 40% of triggered observations are followed by positive SPX returns.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS growth is strong and UNRATE is falling, payroll gains confirm resilient labor income and consumption capacity, which should produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint payroll-unemployment pattern does not outperform single-series versions, or if triggered episodes fail to generate positive 20-day returns with at least 55% hit rate.

mechanism_narrative: A high FEDFUNDS rate combined with elevated CPIAUCSL signals restrictive policy against inflation, raising real discount rates and compressing equity valuations, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS above 4.5% and CPIAUCSL inflation remains above its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if high-rate, high-inflation episodes fail to produce negative 5-day returns or if the signal is not stronger than either variable alone.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, rising GDP and retail sales confirm demand normalization and should lift equities over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations recover.
failure_condition: Reject if joint GDP and retail-sales upswings do not precede positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the average forward return is not materially above the unconditional benchmark.
